methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Synhelion, a Series C climate and energy technology company founded by Gianluca Ambrosetti, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 private firms and 1 public peer in the Climate & Energy sector. The three most proximate comparables—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—establish a $700M to $2.0B range.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to Synhelion's $80M+ in total raised capital and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-later advancement, yields $136M. Peer-median valuation, based on the three same-stage, same-sector comparables listed above, produces $1.0B as the central estimate, with observed range of $700M to $2.0B.

Synthesizing these approaches produces a valuation range of $116M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a triangulated median of $568M. The wide dispersion—spanning approximately one order of magnitude—reflects material uncertainty around whether Synhelion's technology maturity, capital efficiency, and pathway to commercial scale align with peers at the $1B+ band or occupy a more conservative position relative to its funding history. Revenue traction, path to cash flow positivity, and technology de-risking outcomes remain the primary variables that would narrow this range.
